meta.domain_hint: Generic protocol change rules for structured messages/packets (protocol-agnostic)

changes.change_condition: After the Server assigns a unique ClientID for a zero-length ClientID case
changes.evidence: It MUST then process the CONNECT packet as if the Client had provided that unique ClientID, and MUST return the Assigned Client Identifier in the CONNACK packet.

changes.change_condition: If the Client connects using a zero length Client Identifier
changes.change_action: set to a new Client Identifier not used by any other Session currently in the Server
changes.new_value: a new Client Identifier not used by any other Session currently in the Server
changes.evidence: The Assigned Client Identifier MUST be a new Client Identifier not used by any other Session currently in the Server

changes.change_condition: If the Client connects using a zero length Client Identifier
changes.change_action: must be present in CONNACK and must equal a new Client Identifier not used by any other Session currently in the Server
changes.new_value: a new Client Identifier not used by any other Session currently in the Server
changes.evidence: If the Client connects using a zero length Client Identifier, the Server MUST respond with a CONNACK containing an Assigned Client Identifier. The Assigned Client Identifier MUST be a new Client Identifier not used by any other Session currently in the Server.

changes.change_condition: Server assigns a unique ClientID after zero-length ClientID was supplied
changes.new_value: the assigned unique ClientID returned in the CONNACK packet
changes.evidence: and MUST return the Assigned Client Identifier in the CONNACK packet [MQTT-3.1.3-7].

changes.change_condition: in cases where the ClientID is assigned by the Server
changes.evidence: In cases where the ClientID is assigned by the Server, return the assigned ClientID.

changes.change_condition: when included more than once
changes.evidence: It is a Protocol Error to include the Assigned Client Identifier more than once.

changes.change_condition: Client initiates a re-authentication after receiving a CONNACK
changes.evidence: It does this by sending an AUTH packet with a Reason Code of 0x19 (Re-authentication).

changes.change_condition: Client sends additional authentication data during re-authentication
changes.evidence: The Client can respond with additional authentication data by sending an AUTH packet with a Reason Code of 0x18 (Continue authentication).

changes.change_condition: If the Client supplied an Authentication Method in the CONNECT packet and initiates re-authentication
changes.related_state_or_step: AUTH packet sent by Client after receiving a CONNACK
changes.evidence: by sending an AUTH packet with a Reason Code of 0x19 (Re-authentication).

changes.change_condition: If the Client supplied an Authentication Method in the CONNECT packet and initiates re-authentication
changes.change_action: must equal original authentication method value
changes.new_value: same value as the Authentication Method originally used to authenticate the Network Connection
changes.related_state_or_step: AUTH packet sent by Client for re-authentication
changes.evidence: The Client MUST set the Authentication Method to the same value as the Authentication Method originally used to authenticate the Network Connection.
changes.note: Authentication Method property is not in the catalog; incomplete information

changes.change_condition: If the Reason Code and Property Length are omitted and there are no Properties
changes.evidence: The Reason Code and Property Length can be omitted if the Reason Code is 0x00 (Success) and there are no Properties.

changes.change_condition: If the Server requires additional information to complete the authorization
changes.evidence: This packet MUST contain a Reason Code of 0x18 (Continue authentication).

changes.change_condition: If the initial CONNECT packet included an Authentication Method property
changes.change_action: must be present with same value as in CONNECT packet
changes.new_value: same value as in the CONNECT packet
changes.evidence: all AUTH packets, and any successful CONNACK packet MUST include an Authentication Method Property with the same value as in the CONNECT packet.
changes.note: Authentication Method property is not in the catalog; mapped only as an AUTH-related field reference with incomplete information

changes.change_condition: If the initial CONNECT packet included an Authentication Method property, then for all AUTH packets
changes.change_action: must be present with paired Authentication Method rule in same packets
changes.evidence: If the initial CONNECT packet included an Authentication Method property then all AUTH packets, and any successful CONNACK packet MUST include an Authentication Method Property with the same value as in the CONNECT packet

changes.change_condition: Sender of the AUTH Packet
changes.evidence: The sender of the AUTH Packet MUST use one of the Authenticate Reason Codes

changes.change_condition: Server responds to a re-authentication request indicating more authentication data is needed
changes.evidence: or a Reason Code of 0x18 (Continue authentication) to indicate that more authentication data is needed.

changes.change_condition: Server responds to a re-authentication request indicating re-authentication is complete
changes.evidence: The Server responds to this re-authentication request by sending an AUTH packet to the Client with a Reason Code of 0x00 (Success) to indicate that the re-authentication is complete

changes.change_condition: When the Client responds to an AUTH packet from the Server
changes.evidence: This packet MUST contain a Reason Code of 0x18 (Continue authentication).

changes.change_condition: when sending an AUTH packet
changes.new_value: one of the Authenticate Reason Codes
changes.evidence: The sender of the AUTH Packet MUST use one of the Authenticate Reason Codes.

changes.change_condition: When sending MQTT Control Packets in WebSocket transport
changes.change_action: must be sent in binary data frames
changes.evidence: MQTT Control Packets MUST be sent in WebSocket binary data frames.

changes.change_condition: If the value is 1
changes.evidence: If the value is 1, Application Messages MUST NOT be forwarded to a connection with a ClientID equal to the ClientID of the publishing connection.

changes.change_condition: Shared Subscription
changes.evidence: It is a Protocol Error to set the No Local bit to 1 on a Shared Subscription.

changes.change_condition: Subscription Options field
changes.evidence: Bit 2 of the Subscription Options represents the No Local option.

changes.change_condition: PUBREL packet Fixed Header
changes.evidence: Bits 3,2,1 and 0 of the Fixed Header in the PUBREL packet are reserved and MUST be set to 0,0,1 and 0 respectively.

changes.change_condition: PUBREL packet Fixed Header bits have any value other than 0,0,1,0
changes.evidence: The Server MUST treat any other value as malformed and close the Network Connection.

changes.change_condition: SUBSCRIBE packet Fixed Header
changes.evidence: Bits 3,2,1 and 0 of the Fixed Header of the SUBSCRIBE packet are reserved and MUST be set to 0,0,1 and 0 respectively.

changes.change_condition: SUBSCRIBE packet Fixed Header bits have any value other than 0,0,1,0
changes.evidence: The Server MUST treat any other value as malformed and close the Network Connection

changes.change_condition: UNSUBSCRIBE packet Fixed Header
changes.evidence: Bits 3,2,1 and 0 of the Fixed Header of the UNSUBSCRIBE packet are reserved and MUST be set to 0,0,1 and 0 respectively.

changes.change_condition: UNSUBSCRIBE packet Fixed Header contains any value other than 0,0,1,0
changes.evidence: The Server MUST treat any other value as malformed and close the Network Connection

changes.change_condition: for the Fixed Header of the SUBSCRIBE packet
changes.evidence: Bits 3,2,1 and 0 of the Fixed Header of the SUBSCRIBE packet are reserved and MUST be set to 0,0,1 and 0 respectively.

changes.change_condition: if any value other than 0,0,1,0 is used in the Fixed Header of the SUBSCRIBE packet
changes.evidence: The Server MUST treat any other value as malformed and close the Network Connection

changes.change_condition: if the Fixed Header bits of the AUTH packet are any value other than all 0
changes.evidence: The Client or Server MUST treat any other value as malformed and close the Network Connection.

changes.change_condition: if the Fixed Header bits of the UNSUBSCRIBE packet are any value other than 0,0,1,0
changes.evidence: The Server MUST treat any other value as malformed and close the Network Connection

changes.change_condition: in the Fixed Header of a PUBREL packet
changes.evidence: Bits 3,2,1 and 0 of the Fixed Header in the PUBREL packet are reserved and MUST be set to 0,0,1 and 0 respectively.

changes.change_condition: in the Fixed Header of the AUTH packet
changes.evidence: Bits 3,2,1 and 0 of the Fixed Header of the AUTH packet are reserved and MUST all be set to 0.

changes.change_condition: in the Fixed Header of the UNSUBSCRIBE packet
changes.evidence: Bits 3,2,1 and 0 of the Fixed Header of the UNSUBSCRIBE packet are reserved and MUST be set to 0,0,1 and 0 respectively.

changes.change_condition: value is not 0,0,1,0 in the Fixed Header of a PUBREL packet
changes.evidence: The Server MUST treat any other value as malformed and close the Network Connection

changes.change_condition: For equivalence mapping from MQTT v5.0 settings
changes.evidence: Setting of Clean Start to 1 and Session Expiry Interval to 0 is equivalent in MQTT v3.1.1 of setting Clean Session to 1.

changes.change_condition: A Client that only wants to process messages while connected
changes.evidence: A Client that only wants to process messages while connected will set the Clean Start to 1 and set the Session Expiry Interval to 0.

changes.change_condition: For Reason Code 0x91 (Packet identifier in use)
changes.related_state_or_step: reset the Session state by connecting
changes.evidence: For Reason Code 0x91 (Packet identifier in use), the response to this is either to try to fix the state, or to reset the Session state by connecting using Clean Start set to 1

changes.change_condition: For equivalence with MQTT v3.1.1 Clean Session = 1
changes.evidence: Setting of Clean Start to 1 and Session Expiry Interval to 0 is equivalent in MQTT v3.1.1 of setting Clean Session to 1.

changes.change_condition: If a CONNECT packet is received with Clean Start is set to 1
changes.evidence: If a CONNECT packet is received with Clean Start is set to 1

changes.change_condition: If a CONNECT packet is received with Clean Start set to 0 and there is a Session associated with the Client Identifier
changes.evidence: If a CONNECT packet is received with Clean Start set to 0 and there is a Session associated with the Client Identifier

changes.change_condition: If a CONNECT packet is received with Clean Start set to 0 and there is no Session associated with the Client Identifier
changes.evidence: If a CONNECT packet is received with Clean Start set to 0 and there is no Session associated with the Client Identifier

changes.change_condition: If accepted connection has Clean Start set to 0
changes.evidence: If the Server accepts a connection with Clean Start set to 0

changes.change_condition: If accepted connection has Clean Start set to 1
changes.evidence: If the Server accepts a connection with Clean Start set to 1

changes.change_condition: Setting Clean Start to 0 and no Session Expiry Interval
changes.evidence: Setting Clean Start to 0 and no Session Expiry Interval, is equivalent to setting CleanSession to 0 in the MQTT Specification Version 3.1.1.

changes.change_condition: Setting Clean Start to 1 and a Session Expiry Interval of 0
changes.evidence: Setting Clean Start to 1 and a Session Expiry Interval of 0, is equivalent to setting CleanSession to 1 in the MQTT Specification Version 3.1.1.

changes.change_condition: When a Client reconnects
changes.evidence: When a Client reconnects with Clean Start set to 0

changes.change_condition: if a CONNECT packet is received
changes.evidence: If a CONNECT packet is received with Clean Start is set to 1

changes.change_condition: if a CONNECT packet is received and there is a Session associated with the Client Identifier
changes.evidence: If a CONNECT packet is received with Clean Start set to 0 and there is a Session associated with the Client Identifier

changes.change_condition: if a CONNECT packet is received and there is no Session associated with the Client Identifier
changes.evidence: If a CONNECT packet is received with Clean Start set to 0 and there is no Session associated with the Client Identifier

changes.change_condition: processing a validated CONNECT packet
changes.evidence: The Server MUST perform the processing of Clean Start

changes.change_condition: when a Client reconnects and a session is present
changes.evidence: When a Client reconnects with Clean Start set to 0 and a session is present

changes.change_condition: Before accepting a Client connection after successful authentication
changes.change_action: must be checked that the Client is authorized to use it
changes.related_state_or_step: authorization of Clients by the Server
changes.evidence: In particular, the implementation should check that the Client is authorized to use the Client Identifier as this gives access to the MQTT Session State

changes.change_condition: Bit 2 value is 1 for the No Local option
changes.related_state_or_step: Forwarding Application Messages under No Local option
changes.evidence: If the value is 1, Application Messages MUST NOT be forwarded to a connection with a ClientID equal to the ClientID of the publishing connection.

changes.change_condition: CONNECT Payload specification
changes.evidence: They specify a unique Client identifier for the Client, a Will Topic, Will Payload, User Name and Password. All but the Client identifier can be omitted

changes.change_condition: CONNECT packet Payload
changes.related_state_or_step: CONNECT Payload field ordering and inclusion
changes.evidence: The ClientID MUST be present and is the first field in the CONNECT packet Payload [MQTT-3.1.3-3].

changes.change_condition: Client Identifier encoding requirement
changes.evidence: The ClientID MUST be a UTF-8 Encoded String as defined in section 1.5.4 [MQTT-3.1.3-4].

changes.change_condition: Client supplies a ClientID that has a length of zero bytes and Server allows it
changes.evidence: A Server MAY allow a Client to supply a ClientID that has a length of zero bytes, however if it does so the Server MUST treat this as a special case and assign a unique ClientID to that Client [MQTT-3.1.3-6].

changes.change_condition: If the Client connects using a zero length Client Identifier
changes.change_action: must be present as Assigned Client Identifier and selected as a new identifier not used by any other Session currently in the Server
changes.new_value: new Client Identifier not used by any other Session currently in the Server
changes.evidence: If the Client connects using a zero length Client Identifier, the Server MUST respond with a CONNACK containing an Assigned Client Identifier. The Assigned Client Identifier MUST be a new Client Identifier not used by any other Session currently in the Server.

changes.change_condition: If the ClientID represents a Client already connected to the Server
changes.change_action: equality check against an already connected client identifier
changes.evidence: If the ClientID represents a Client already connected to the Server

changes.change_condition: If the supplied ClientID has a length of zero bytes and the Server allows it
changes.evidence: A Server MAY allow a Client to supply a ClientID that has a length of zero bytes, however if it does so the Server MUST treat this as a special case and assign a unique ClientID to that Client.

changes.change_condition: In the CONNECT packet Payload
changes.evidence: The ClientID MUST be present and is the first field in the CONNECT packet Payload.

changes.change_condition: Server acceptance of ClientID values
changes.new_value: between 1 and 23 UTF-8 encoded bytes in length
changes.evidence: The Server MUST allow ClientID’s which are between 1 and 23 UTF-8 encoded bytes in length

changes.change_condition: Server acceptance of ClientID values
changes.evidence: and that contain only the characters "0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ" [MQTT-3.1.3-5].

changes.change_condition: Server assigns a unique ClientID after zero-length ClientID was supplied
changes.new_value: the unique ClientID assigned by the Server
changes.evidence: It MUST then process the CONNECT packet as if the Client had provided that unique ClientID [MQTT-3.1.3-7].

changes.change_condition: Server handling of supplied ClientID
changes.new_value: between 1 and 23 UTF-8 encoded bytes in length
changes.evidence: The Server MUST allow ClientID’s which are between 1 and 23 UTF-8 encoded bytes in length

changes.change_condition: Server handling of supplied ClientID
changes.evidence: and that contain only the characters "0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ".

changes.change_condition: Server rejects the ClientID
changes.evidence: If the Server rejects the ClientID it MAY respond to the CONNECT packet with a CONNACK using Reason Code 0x85 (Client Identifier not valid) as described in section 4.13 Handling errors, and then it MUST close the Network Connection [MQTT-3.1.3-8].

changes.change_condition: When No Local value is 1
changes.change_action: must not equal for forwarding eligibility
changes.new_value: must not be forwarded when ClientID equal to the ClientID of the publishing connection
changes.evidence: If the value is 1, Application Messages MUST NOT be forwarded to a connection with a ClientID equal to the ClientID of the publishing connection [MQTT-3.8.3-3].

changes.change_condition: When carried as ClientID
changes.evidence: The ClientID MUST be a UTF-8 Encoded String.

changes.change_condition: a zero length Client Identifier was found in the CONNECT packet
changes.change_action: must be present as Assigned Client Identifier in CONNACK
changes.evidence: If the Client connects using a zero length Client Identifier, the Server MUST respond with a CONNACK containing an Assigned Client Identifier.

changes.change_condition: another Connection using the same ClientID has connected
changes.evidence: Another Connection using the same ClientID has connected causing this Connection to be closed.

changes.change_condition: when a Client provides a Client Identifier
changes.change_action: must not equal a Client Identifier already being used by some other Client
changes.evidence: This authorization check is to protect against the case where one Client, accidentally or maliciously, provides a Client Identifier that is already being used by some other Client.

changes.change_condition: If Will QoS exceeds Server capabilities and the Server uses CONNACK
changes.evidence: It SHOULD use a CONNACK packet with Reason Code 0x9B (QoS not supported)

changes.change_condition: If Will Retain is 1 and retained messages are not supported
changes.evidence: It SHOULD send CONNACK with Reason Code 0x9A (Retain not supported)

changes.change_condition: If a Server sends a CONNACK packet containing a Reason code of 0x80 or greater
changes.evidence: If a Server sends a CONNACK packet containing a Reason code of 0x80 or greater

changes.change_condition: If a Server sends a CONNACK packet containing a non-zero Reason Code
changes.evidence: If a Server sends a CONNACK packet containing a non-zero Reason Code

changes.change_condition: If a well formed CONNECT packet is received by the Server, but the Server is unable to complete the Connection.
changes.new_value: the appropriate Connect Reason code from this table
changes.evidence: the Server MAY send a CONNACK packet containing the appropriate Connect Reason code from this table

changes.change_condition: If the Protocol Version is not 5 and the Server does not want to accept the CONNECT packet
changes.evidence: the Server MAY send a CONNACK packet with Reason Code 0x84 (Unsupported Protocol Version)

changes.change_condition: If the Server accepts a connection with Clean Start set to 1
changes.evidence: in addition to setting a 0x00 (Success) Reason Code in the CONNACK packet.

changes.change_condition: If the Server does not want to accept the CONNECT, and wishes to reveal that it is an MQTT Server
changes.evidence: it MAY send a CONNACK packet with Reason Code of 0x84 (Unsupported Protocol Version)

changes.change_condition: If the Server rejects the ClientID
changes.evidence: If the Server rejects the ClientID it MAY respond to the CONNECT packet with a CONNACK using Reason Code 0x85 (Client Identifier not valid)

changes.change_condition: If the initial CONNECT packet included an Authentication Method property
changes.change_action: must include same Authentication Method value as in CONNECT
changes.new_value: same value as in the CONNECT packet
changes.evidence: any successful CONNACK packet MUST include an Authentication Method Property with the same value as in the CONNECT packet.
changes.note: Authentication Method property is not in the catalog; incomplete information

changes.change_condition: In both Clean Start set to 0 cases
changes.evidence: In both cases it MUST set a 0x00 (Success) Reason Code in the CONNACK packet.

changes.change_condition: Server detects a Malformed Packet or Protocol Error in a CONNECT packet and sends CONNACK before closing the Network Connection
changes.change_action: set to selected from offered list
changes.new_value: 0x81 (Malformed Packet) or 0x82 (Protocol Error) unless a more specific Reason Code has been defined
changes.evidence: Use Reason Code 0x81 (Malformed Packet) or 0x82 (Protocol Error) unless a more specific Reason Code has been defined in section 3.2.2.2 - Connect Reason Code

changes.change_condition: When the Server acknowledges the CONNECT packet
changes.evidence: The Server MUST acknowledge the CONNECT packet with a CONNACK packet containing a 0x00 (Success) Reason Code.

changes.change_condition: When the Server sends the CONNACK packet
changes.new_value: one of the Connect Reason Code values
changes.evidence: The Server sending the CONNACK packet MUST use one of the Connect Reason Code values.

changes.change_condition: before a Server sends a DISCONNECT packet
changes.evidence: A Server MUST NOT send a DISCONNECT until after it has sent a CONNACK with Reason Code of less than 0x80

changes.change_condition: if a Server receives a CONNECT packet containing a Will Message with the Will Retain set to 1, and it does not support retained messages
changes.evidence: It SHOULD send CONNACK with Reason Code 0x9A (Retain not supported)

changes.change_condition: if a Server receives a CONNECT packet containing a Will QoS that exceeds its capabilities
changes.evidence: It SHOULD use a CONNACK packet with Reason Code 0x9B (QoS not supported)

changes.change_condition: when the Server acknowledges a validated CONNECT packet with CONNACK
changes.evidence: The Server MUST acknowledge the CONNECT packet with a CONNACK packet containing a 0x00 (Success) Reason Code

changes.change_condition: when the Server sends the CONNACK packet
changes.change_action: must use one of the Connect Reason Code values
changes.new_value: one of the Connect Reason Code values
changes.evidence: The Server sending the CONNACK packet MUST use one of the Connect Reason Code values

changes.change_condition: When a message is published
changes.related_state_or_step: Payload format and content type feature summary
changes.evidence: Allow the payload format (binary, text) and a MIME style content type to be specified when a message is published.

changes.change_condition: in a PUBLISH packet
changes.change_action: must be a UTF-8 Encoded String
changes.evidence: The Content Type MUST be a UTF-8 Encoded String.

changes.change_condition: when Content Type appears more than once
changes.evidence: It is a Protocol Error to include the Content Type more than once.

changes.change_condition: when Content Type is included
changes.change_action: must be valid UTF-8 Encoded String
changes.evidence: The Content Type MUST be a UTF-8 Encoded String as defined in section 1.5.4

changes.change_condition: when Content Type is included in the message
changes.change_action: must not be present more than once; invalid if value check fails
changes.evidence: It is a Protocol Error to include the Content Type more than once.

changes.change_condition: when a Server sends the Application Message to subscribers
changes.evidence: A Server MUST send the Content Type unaltered to all subscribers receiving the Application Message

changes.change_condition: when the Server sends to all subscribers receiving the Application Message
changes.evidence: A Server MUST send the Content Type unaltered to all subscribers receiving the Application Message.

changes.change_condition: if X > 0 during Variable Byte Integer encoding
changes.evidence:
if (X > 0)
encodedByte = encodedByte OR 128

changes.change_condition: within each byte of Variable Byte Integer encoding

changes.change_condition: Application Message contains both a Response Topic and a Correlation Data and receiver sends a response
changes.new_value: same Correlation Data in response PUBLISH
changes.evidence: The Client should also send the Correlation Data unaltered as part of the PUBLISH of the responses.

changes.change_condition: For request/response pattern
changes.change_action: provided to allow routing of response messages
changes.evidence: provide the Response Topic and Correlation Data properties to allow response messages to be routed back to the publisher of a request.

changes.change_condition: Server forwards the Application Message to subscribers
changes.old_value: Correlation Data in received Application Message
changes.evidence: The Server MUST send the Correlation Data unaltered to all subscribers receiving the Application Message [MQTT-3.3.2-16].

changes.change_condition: The MQTT Server forwards the Request Message or the Response Message
changes.old_value: Correlation Data Property in the Request Message or Correlation Data in the Response Message
changes.evidence: The MQTT Server forwards the Response Topic and Correlation Data Property in the Request Message and the Correlation Data in the Response Message.

changes.change_condition: The Request contains a Correlation Data property
changes.old_value: Correlation Data property in the Request Message
changes.new_value: same Correlation Data property in the Response Message

changes.change_condition: included more than once
changes.evidence: It is a Protocol Error to include Correlation Data more than once.

changes.change_condition: when Correlation Data is included in the message
changes.change_action: must not be present more than once; invalid if value check fails
changes.evidence: It is a Protocol Error to include Correlation Data more than once.

changes.change_condition: when the Server sends to all subscribers receiving the Application Message
changes.evidence: The Server MUST send the Correlation Data unaltered to all subscribers receiving the Application Message.

changes.change_condition: Client receives more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets where it has not sent a PUBACK or PUBCOMP in response
changes.evidence: the Client uses DISCONNECT with Reason Code 0x93 (Receive Maximum exceeded)

changes.change_condition: If Retain Available was included in CONNACK with value 0 and the Server receives a PUBLISH packet with RETAIN flag set to 1
changes.evidence: then it uses the DISCONNECT Reason Code of 0x9A (Retain not supported)

changes.change_condition: If the ClientID represents a Client already connected to the Server
changes.related_state_or_step: Server sends a DISCONNECT packet to the existing Client
changes.evidence: the Server sends a DISCONNECT packet to the existing Client with Reason Code of 0x8E (Session taken over)

changes.change_condition: If the receiver validates the Payload format and it is not of the format indicated
changes.evidence: if it is not send a PUBACK, PUBREC, or DISCONNECT with Reason Code of 0x99 (Payload format invalid)

changes.change_condition: On receipt of DISCONNECT with a Reason Code of 0x00 (Success)

changes.change_condition: Server detects a Malformed Packet or Protocol Error in any packet other than CONNECT and sends DISCONNECT before closing the Network Connection
changes.change_action: set to selected from offered list
changes.new_value: 0x81 (Malformed Packet) or 0x82 (Protocol Error) unless a more specific Reason Code has been defined
changes.evidence: Use Reason Code 0x81 (Malformed Packet) or 0x82 (Protocol Error) unless a more specific Reason Code has been defined in section 3.14.2.1 – Disconnect Reason Code.

changes.change_condition: Server receives more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets where it has not sent a PUBACK or PUBCOMP in response
changes.related_state_or_step: Server error handling using DISCONNECT packet
changes.evidence: the Server uses a DISCONNECT packet with Reason Code 0x93 (Receive Maximum exceeded)

changes.change_condition: if reserved bits are not zero in the DISCONNECT Fixed Header
changes.evidence: If they are not zero it sends a DISCONNECT packet with a Reason code of 0x81 (Malformed Packet)

changes.change_condition: if the Network Connection is closed without the Client first sending a DISCONNECT packet with Reason Code 0x00 (Normal disconnection)
changes.evidence: If the Network Connection is closed without the Client first sending a DISCONNECT packet with Reason Code 0x00 (Normal disconnection)

changes.change_condition: if the Remaining Length is less than 1
changes.evidence: If the Remaining Length is less than 1 the value of 0x00 (Normal disconnection) is used.

changes.change_condition: when sending a DISCONNECT packet
changes.new_value: one of the DISCONNECT Reason Codes
changes.evidence: The Client or Server sending the DISCONNECT packet MUST use one of the DISCONNECT Reason Codes.

changes.change_condition: After the receiver has sent a PUBACK packet and an incoming PUBLISH packet contains the same Packet Identifier
changes.change_action: validity check ignored for this judgment
changes.related_state_or_step: Receiver judgment for subsequent incoming PUBLISH packets
changes.evidence: irrespective of the setting of its DUP flag

changes.change_condition: In the QoS 0 delivery protocol, when the sender sends a PUBLISH packet
changes.evidence: MUST send a PUBLISH packet with QoS 0 and DUP flag set to 0

changes.change_condition: In the QoS 1 delivery protocol, when the sender sends a PUBLISH packet
changes.evidence: MUST send a PUBLISH packet containing this Packet Identifier with QoS 1 and DUP flag set to 0

changes.change_condition: when sending a PUBLISH packet for QoS 2
changes.evidence: MUST send a PUBLISH packet containing this Packet Identifier with QoS 2 and DUP flag set to 0

changes.change_condition: In the QoS 2 delivery protocol, when the sender sends the PUBLISH packet for the new Application Message
changes.evidence: the sender MUST send a PUBLISH packet containing this Packet Identifier with QoS 2 and DUP flag set to 0

changes.change_condition: for all QoS 0 messages
changes.evidence: The DUP flag MUST be set to 0 for all QoS 0 messages

changes.change_condition: for an outgoing PUBLISH packet
changes.change_action: must be determined solely by whether the outgoing PUBLISH packet is a retransmission and set independently of the incoming PUBLISH packet
changes.old_value: incoming PUBLISH packet DUP flag value
changes.new_value: value determined solely by whether the outgoing PUBLISH packet is a retransmission
changes.evidence: The DUP flag in the outgoing PUBLISH packet is set independently to the incoming PUBLISH packet, its value MUST be determined solely by whether the outgoing PUBLISH packet is a retransmission.

changes.change_condition: in the QoS 0 delivery protocol when sending a PUBLISH packet
changes.evidence: In the QoS 0 delivery protocol, the sender MUST send a PUBLISH packet with QoS 0 and DUP flag set to 0.

changes.change_condition: in the QoS 1 delivery protocol when sending a PUBLISH packet
changes.evidence: In the QoS 1 delivery protocol, the sender MUST send a PUBLISH packet containing this Packet Identifier with QoS 1 and DUP flag set to 0.

changes.change_condition: when the Client or Server attempts to re-deliver a PUBLISH packet
changes.evidence: The DUP flag MUST be set to 1 by the Client or Server when it attempts to re-deliver a PUBLISH packet

changes.change_condition: when the Server sends an outgoing PUBLISH packet to subscribers
changes.change_action: must not be derived/computed from another field
changes.new_value: determined solely by whether the outgoing PUBLISH packet is a retransmission

changes.change_action: validated check that the Disallowed Unicode code points are not used
changes.related_state_or_step: Considerations for the use of Disallowed Unicode code points
changes.evidence: An implementation would normally choose to validate UTF-8 Encoded strings, checking that the Disallowed Unicode code points are not used.

changes.change_condition: when a UTF-8 encoded sequence 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF appears in a string
changes.evidence: A UTF-8 encoded sequence 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF is always interpreted as U+FEFF ("ZERO WIDTH NO-BREAK SPACE") wherever it appears in a string

changes.change_condition: when a UTF-8 encoded sequence 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF appears in a string
changes.new_value: not skipped over or stripped off
changes.evidence: and MUST NOT be skipped over or stripped off by a packet receiver [MQTT-1.5.4-3].

changes.change_condition: when character data is carried in a UTF-8 Encoded String
changes.change_action: must be valid UTF-8 and must not include encodings of code points between U+D800 and U+DFFF
changes.evidence: The character data in a UTF-8 Encoded String MUST be well-formed UTF-8 as defined by the Unicode specification [Unicode] and restated in RFC 3629 [RFC3629]. In particular, the character data MUST NOT include encodings of code points between U+D800 and U+DFFF.

changes.change_condition: when the UTF-8 encoded sequence 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF appears in a string
changes.change_action: must be interpreted as U+FEFF and must not be skipped over or stripped off by a packet receiver
changes.new_value: interpreted as U+FEFF ("ZERO WIDTH NO-BREAK SPACE")
changes.evidence: A UTF-8 encoded sequence 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF is always interpreted as U+FEFF ("ZERO WIDTH NO-BREAK SPACE") wherever it appears in a string and MUST NOT be skipped over or stripped off by a packet receiver.

changes.change_condition: when validating UTF-8 Encoded Strings
changes.change_action: must not include Disallowed Unicode code points
changes.related_state_or_step: Handling of Disallowed Unicode code points
changes.evidence: Section 1.5.4 describes the Disallowed Unicode code points, which should not be included in a UTF-8 Encoded String.

changes.change_condition: when validating a UTF-8 Encoded String
changes.change_action: must not include an encoding of the null character U+0000
changes.evidence: A UTF-8 Encoded String MUST NOT include an encoding of the null character U+0000.

changes.change_condition: If a receiver (Client or Server) receives a string pair which does not meet these requirements
changes.evidence: If a receiver (Client or Server) receives a string pair which does not meet these requirements it is a Malformed Packet.

changes.change_condition: when validating both strings in the pair
changes.change_action: both strings must comply with the requirements for UTF-8 Encoded Strings
changes.evidence: Both strings MUST comply with the requirements for UTF-8 Encoded Strings.

changes.change_condition: for a UTF-8 String Pair
changes.evidence: The first string serves as the name, and the second string contains the value. Both strings MUST comply with the requirements for UTF-8 Encoded Strings [MQTT-1.5.7-1].

changes.change_condition: during decoding if multiplier > 128*128*128
changes.evidence:
if (multiplier > 128*128*128)
throw Error(Malformed Variable Byte Integer)

changes.change_condition: for the Variable Byte Integer field
changes.new_value: maximum number of bytes is four
changes.evidence: The maximum number of bytes in the Variable Byte Integer field is four.

changes.change_condition: for values up to 127
changes.evidence: The Variable Byte Integer is encoded using an encoding scheme which uses a single byte for values up to 127.

changes.change_condition: when encoding a value
changes.change_action: must use the minimum number of bytes necessary to represent the value
changes.evidence: The encoded value MUST use the minimum number of bytes necessary to represent the value.

changes.change_condition: when encoding the value
changes.new_value: minimum number of bytes necessary to represent the value
changes.evidence: The encoded value MUST use the minimum number of bytes necessary to represent the value [MQTT-1.5.5-1].

changes.change_condition: If the Protocol Version is not 5 and the Server does not want to accept the CONNECT packet
changes.evidence: If the Protocol Version is not 5 and the Server does not want to accept the CONNECT packet

changes.change_condition: Protocol Version field for version 5.0 of the protocol
changes.evidence: The value of the Protocol Version field for version 5.0 of the protocol is 5 (0x05).

changes.change_condition: when validating CONNECT
changes.evidence: If the Protocol Version is not 5 and the Server does not want to accept the CONNECT packet

changes.change_condition: If present
changes.evidence: A value is 0 means that Wildcard Subscriptions are not supported. A value of 1 means Wildcard Subscriptions are supported.

changes.change_condition: if the Client uses features that the Server has declared are not available
changes.related_state_or_step: client use of unavailable server-declared feature
changes.evidence: It is an error for the Client to use features that the Server has declared are not available.
changes.note: applies when Wildcard Subscription Available is one of the declared unavailable features

changes.change_condition: included more than once or sent with a value other than 0 or 1
changes.evidence: It is a Protocol Error to include the Wildcard Subscription Available more than once or to send a value other than 0 or 1.

changes.change_condition: not present
changes.evidence: If not present, then Wildcard Subscriptions are supported.

changes.change_condition: when the Server specifies features it does not allow
changes.change_action: set to indicate feature availability not available
changes.new_value: declared not available by the Server
changes.evidence: The features which can be specified in this way are: Maximum QoS, Retain Available, Wildcard Subscription Available, Subscription Identifier Available, and Shared Subscription Available.

changes.change_condition: Will Delay Interval included multiple times
changes.evidence: It is a Protocol Error to include the Will Delay Interval more than once.

changes.change_condition: Will Delay Interval is absent
changes.evidence: If the Will Delay Interval is absent, the default value is 0

changes.change_condition: when specifying a delay between the end of the connection and sending the will message
changes.new_value: a delay between the end of the connection and sending the will message
changes.evidence: Add the ability to specify a delay between the end of the connection and sending the will message.

changes.change_condition: If the Will Flag is set to 1
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1 this indicates that a Will Message MUST be stored on the Server and associated with the Session

changes.change_condition: Will Properties presence in CONNECT Payload
changes.change_action: must equal 1 for field to be present
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1, the Will Properties is the next field in the Payload.

changes.change_condition: if Will QoS and Will Retain are used and Will Properties, Will Topic and Will Message are required
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1, the Will QoS and Will Retain fields in the Connect Flags will be used by the Server, and the Will Properties, Will Topic and Will Message fields MUST be present in the Payload.

changes.change_condition: when indicating a Will Message
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1 this indicates that, a Will Message MUST be stored on the Server and associated with the Session.

changes.change_condition: If the Will Flag is set to 1
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1, the Will Properties, Will Topic, and Will Payload fields MUST be present in the Payload

changes.change_condition: If the Will Flag is set to 1
changes.change_action: must be present as the next field in the Payload; must equal required type/format
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1 the Will Payload is the next field in the Payload. This field consists of Binary Data.

changes.change_condition: If the Will Flag is set to 1
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1, the Will Properties, Will Topic, and Will Payload fields MUST be present in the Payload

changes.change_condition: If a Server receives a CONNECT packet containing a Will QoS that exceeds its capabilities
changes.evidence: If a Server receives a CONNECT packet containing a Will QoS that exceeds its capabilities, it MUST reject the connection.

changes.change_condition: If the Will Flag is set to 0
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 0, then the Will QoS MUST be set to 0 (0x00)

changes.change_condition: If the Will Flag is set to 1
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1, the value of Will QoS can be 0 (0x00), 1 (0x01), or 2 (0x02)

changes.change_condition: if a Server receives a CONNECT packet containing a Will QoS that exceeds its capabilities
changes.evidence: If a Server receives a CONNECT packet containing a Will QoS that exceeds its capabilities, it MUST reject the connection.

changes.change_condition: if the Will Flag is set to 0
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 0, then the Will QoS MUST be set to 0 (0x00).

changes.change_condition: if the Will Flag is set to 1
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1, the value of Will QoS can be 0 (0x00), 1 (0x01), or 2 (0x02).

changes.change_condition: when value is 3 (0x03)
changes.evidence: A value of 3 (0x03) is a Malformed Packet.

changes.change_condition: If a Server receives a CONNECT packet containing a Will Message with the Will Retain 1, and it does not support retained messages

changes.change_condition: If the Will Flag is set to 0
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 0, then Will Retain MUST be set to 0

changes.change_condition: If the Will Flag is set to 1 and Will Retain is set to 0
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1 and Will Retain is set to 0, the Server MUST publish the Will Message as a non-retained message

changes.change_condition: If the Will Flag is set to 1 and Will Retain is set to 1
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1 and Will Retain is set to 1, the Server MUST publish the Will Message as a retained message

changes.change_condition: if a Server receives a CONNECT packet containing a Will Message with the Will Retain set to 1, and it does not support retained messages

changes.evidence: If a Server receives a CONNECT packet containing a Will Message with the Will Retain set to 1, and it does not support retained messages, the Server MUST reject the connection request.

changes.change_condition: if the Will Flag is set to 0
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 0, then Will Retain MUST be set to 0.

changes.change_condition: if the Will Flag is set to 1 and publishing behavior depends on retained status
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1 and Will Retain is set to 0, the Server MUST publish the Will Message as a non-retained message.

changes.change_condition: if the Will Flag is set to 1 and publishing behavior depends on retained status
changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1 and Will Retain is set to 1, the Server MUST publish the Will Message as a retained message.

changes.evidence: If the Will Flag is set to 1, the Will Topic is the next field in the Payload. The Will Topic MUST be a UTF-8 Encoded String
